title: PermissionManager::unrestricted() bypasses safety-path checks that CC enforces even in bypassPermissions mode
description:
## Summary

CC's bypassPermissions mode (utils/permissions/permissions.ts:1266-1281) still enforces:
- Step 1g: safetyCheck decisions (writes to .git/, .claude/, .vscode/, shell configs must prompt)
- Step 1e: requiresUserInteraction tools (ExitPlanMode, AskUserQuestion) still ask
- Step 1f: content-specific ask rules still fire
- Step 1d: deny rules still fire

OC's PermissionManager::unrestricted() (src/permissions.rs:105-123) sets enabled=false, which short-circuits check() at line 132-134 before ANY of these checks run. A call site using unrestricted() allows:
- Edit to .git/config
- Write to .claude/settings.json
- Write to ~/.zshrc
without any check.

## Security relevance (high)
CC explicitly designed bypassPermissions to still block safety-critical paths. OC's unrestricted() is a superset bypass that removes all protection. Any OC call site that uses unrestricted() instead of bypassPermissions-mode is unintentionally more permissive than CC.

## Behaviors to align
- Add a safety-path check that fires even when enabled=false or in unrestricted()
- OR document that unrestricted() callers must not be used in contexts where safety paths matter
- Add test: unrestricted().check('edit_file', {path: '.git/config'}) currently returns Allowed (document this gap)
- Add test: CC bypassPermissions + .git/config write → still prompts (safetyCheck step 1g)

## Parent issue
#531

## References
- CC: utils/permissions/permissions.ts:1253-1261 (step 1g, safetyCheck bypass-immune)
- CC: utils/permissions/permissions.ts:1230-1236 (step 1e, requiresUserInteraction)
- OC: src/permissions.rs:105-134 (unrestricted + short-circuit)
